findings.message: D1 が `Read` 対象として `track/items/<id>/<layer>-graph...` を ADR 内に直書きしており、SoT Chain の向きが逆になります。計画 ADR では生成済み track 成果物を固定の参照先として書かず、出力種別だけを抽象的に述べるべきです。

Rejected Alternative C says the combined ADR avoids a situation where a new decision says “reconnaissance は加えない”, but D1 and the rest of the ADR explicitly decide to add reconnaissance before catalogue drafting. This contradiction makes the rejection rationale ambiguous; it appears to mean “do not echo reconnaissance results to the orchestrator,” not “do not add reconnaissance.”

`IN-01`/`IN-05` attribute the `--out-suffix d1/d2` reconnaissance sequence to `knowledge/adr/2026-04-25-0530-type-designer-recon-options-defaults.md#D1`, but that ADR introduces the suffix flag only in D2. The spec is collapsing the D1/D2 split and pointing readers at the wrong decision source; D1 should cover the default `depth=1+2 / edges=all` choice, while D2 should own the suffix-based output separation.

IN-05 treats the suffixed two-pass reconnaissance flow as if it were supported by `0530#D1`, but that ADR section only fixes the default depth/edges choices and still uses the shared `<layer>-graph/` path. The `--out-suffix` coexistence comes from `0530#D2`, so this cross-reference set is wrong/incomplete.

S1 says the Rust changes are confined to `apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/graph.rs` and `libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/type_graph_render.rs`, but T001 explicitly requires updating `apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/mod.rs` as well. As written, the scope statement understates the file set needed for the plan.

IN-01 requires the type-designer pipeline to run the suffixed `--out-suffix d1/d2` reconnaissance flow but only cites the child ADR's D1 decision. In `2026-04-25-0530...`, D1 still defines the unsuffixed sequential `<layer>-graph/` procedure, while D2 owns the `--out-suffix` output separation. This leaves the spec pointing readers at the wrong decision source for the required behavior; the same mismatch is reflected in generated AC-01/AC-06 and T005.

The spec maps the suffix-based reconnaissance sequence to the child ADR's D1, but that ADR's D1 still defines suffixless `type-graph` calls, uses the shared `<layer>-graph/` path, and requires reading depth=1 before running depth=2. D2 only introduces `--out-suffix` as a CLI fix, and the ADR even says updating the D1 examples to suffix form is a later reassessment. As written, T005 would update the agent to a procedure that the cited D1 does not actually specify, so the plan and ADR conflict on the canonical reconnaissance flow.

CN-05 explicitly includes the `.gitignore` pattern addition in the bounded `type-graph` change scope, but its task mapping only lists T001-T003 while T004 is the task that performs the `.gitignore` update. This leaves the constraint coverage internally inconsistent: the task-coverage gate claims CN-05 is covered without the task that implements one of CN-05's stated requirements.

GO-04 cites `knowledge/adr/2026-04-25-0530-type-designer-recon-options-defaults.md#D2` as the design basis for changing `.claude/agents/adr-editor.md`, but that D2 decision is about `bin/sotp track type-graph` auto-cleanup and the `--out-suffix` flag. The ADR contains no decision authorizing the adr-editor rule change, so GO-04/IN-06/AC-07/T007 are presented as ADR-backed by an unrelated decision.

AC-01 requires the `--out-suffix d1/d2` reconnaissance sequence, but its ADR trace points only to the child ADR D1 for the child ADR. In `knowledge/adr/2026-04-25-0530-type-designer-recon-options-defaults.md`, D1 defines the depth=1+2 / edges=all two-pass procedure without suffixes; D2 is the decision that introduces `--out-suffix` and free Read ordering. This is a broken decision reference and should include D2, matching IN-01/AC-06.

T005 describes the suffix-bearing sequence as the "child ADR D1 canonical procedure", but child ADR D1's procedure is suffix-less and the suffix/read-order behavior is introduced by D2. This mislabels the source decision for the implementation task; rewrite it as D1+D2 or D2-backed suffix update.

The ADR still describes the reconnaissance flow in `Decision` as writing depth=1 and depth=2 outputs to the same `<layer>-graph/` path and overwriting the first run, but the later D2 section and Consequences now require suffix-separated directories (`<layer>-graph-d1/`, `<layer>-graph-d2/`) that coexist. That leaves two incompatible prescriptions in the same ADR for the same flow, so a reader cannot tell which path behavior is the intended one.

`CN-05` is mapped to `T001`-`T003` only, but the scope text for `CN-05` explicitly includes the `.gitignore` pattern addition implemented by `T004`. As written, the coverage artifact says the constraint is fully covered while one required task is omitted.

The ADR still defines the D1 reconnaissance procedure as suffixless: depth=1 writes to `<layer>-graph/`, must be read before depth=2, and depth=2 overwrites the same path. Later plan artifacts treat the canonical T005/IN-05 procedure as `--out-suffix d1` / `--out-suffix d2` with free read ordering. That leaves the ADR and implementation plan contradicting each other on the canonical reconnaissance flow.

T002 expands `--out-suffix` to flat mode by writing `<layer>-graph-<name>.md`, but the ADR/spec/T004 only plan the `.gitignore` addition for suffix-named directories (`track/items/**/*-graph-*/`). If the flag works with depth=0 as T002/T003 require, the generated flat markdown artefacts are left unignored and AC-08 will not catch them. Either constrain suffix support to clustered output or add the matching flat-file ignore/verification requirement.

The plan/spec artifacts cite this ADR as `#D1`, but the ADR never defines a named D1 decision. It has an unlabeled `## Decision` section and then a named `## Decision (D2)` / `### D2`, so references such as `...recon-options-defaults.md#D1` have no corresponding design decision anchor. This breaks the SoT chain for the D1-backed requirements; add a `### D1: ...` heading or otherwise name the first decision consistently.

The plan treats `--out-suffix <name>` as a raw `Option<String>` and the catalogue explicitly states `TypeGraphRenderOptions` has no validation invariants, even though that suffix is used to build output paths like `<layer>-graph-<name>/`. Without a task/AC requiring rejection of path separators or `..`, an implementation following this plan can write generated files outside the intended track artifact path. Add validation for the suffix before path construction and cover invalid suffixes in the tests.

The plan treats `--out-suffix <name>` as a raw `Option<String>` path fragment and the catalogue explicitly says `TypeGraphRenderOptions` has no validation invariants. Because that value is interpolated into output paths like `<layer>-graph-<name>/`, an implementation following this plan can accept separators or `..` and write generated artifacts outside the intended track directory. Add a validated suffix type or explicit CLI validation plus invalid-suffix tests before path construction.

The ADR still defines D1 as a suffixless flow where depth=2 overwrites the same `<layer>-graph/` path, while the later D2/Consequences and implementation plan treat `--out-suffix d1/d2` with coexisting `<layer>-graph-d1/` and `<layer>-graph-d2/` directories as the canonical reconnaissance procedure. These are conflicting prescriptions for the same agent pipeline, so the plan leaves implementers unsure whether to preserve the D1 read-before-overwrite sequence or replace it with the D2 suffix-based sequence.

T002 requires suffixed flat-mode output (`<layer>-graph-<name>.md`), but T004/IN-07/AC-08 only add and verify the directory ignore pattern `track/items/**/*-graph-*/`. If `--out-suffix` is supported with depth=0 as T002/T003 require, the generated flat markdown artifacts remain tracked unless a matching `*-graph-*.md` ignore/verification requirement is added, or flat-mode suffix support is explicitly removed from scope.

T002/T003 require `--out-suffix` to work in flat mode by writing `<layer>-graph-<name>.md`, but T004/IN-07/AC-08 only add and verify the directory ignore pattern `track/items/**/*-graph-*/`. If depth=0 suffix output is implemented as planned, those generated markdown artifacts remain unignored despite the stated intent to keep suffix-named graph outputs out of version control.

The plan treats `--out-suffix <name>` as a raw `Option<String>` path fragment, and the catalogue explicitly says `TypeGraphRenderOptions` has no validation invariants. Because that value is interpolated into output paths such as `<layer>-graph-<name>/`, an implementation following this plan can accept separators or `..` and write outside the intended track artifact location. The plan needs a validated suffix or explicit CLI validation with invalid-suffix tests.

`spec.json` / `plan.md` now extend `--out-suffix` to flat `<layer>-graph-<name>.md` outputs and add a second `.gitignore` glob, but the governing ADR `knowledge/adr/2026-04-25-0530-type-designer-recon-options-defaults.md#D2` still only describes directory outputs and a single `track/items/**/*-graph-*/` pattern. The decision record and the derived plan no longer describe the same contract, so implementers following the ADR will miss the flat-file behavior and ignore rule.

The infrastructure type catalogue introduces the field as `output_suffix`, but the rest of the plan/spec consistently refer to `out_suffix` for the `TypeGraphRenderOptions` field. That leaves the target API surface ambiguous and risks implementing the wrong property name.

This ADR still hard-codes a sequential reconnaissance order: step 3 must finish before step 4, and the depth=1 output is read before the depth=2 render. That conflicts with the updated plan/spec artifacts, which make the two reads order-free and place them after both `type-graph` invocations. The result is inconsistent implementation guidance for the same feature.

The canonical reconnaissance procedure is internally inconsistent: this decision still requires reading the depth=1 output before running depth=2 (`track/items/<id>/<layer>-graph/` at the step-3/step-4 boundary), but `impl-plan.json` T005 and `spec.json` AC-01/AC-06 now require independent `<layer>-graph-d1/` and `<layer>-graph-d2/` reads in any order. As written, it is unclear whether the sequential constraint is still authoritative or has been superseded by the `--out-suffix` design.

D1 already prescribes the suffixed calls (`--out-suffix d1` / `--out-suffix d2`) and says the depth outputs can be read in either order, but this Reassess When item still says updating D1's step 2/4 examples to suffix form is a future optional decision. That contradicts the current Decision section and leaves the canonical reconnaissance flow ambiguous.

The type catalogue names the new `TypeGraphRenderOptions` field `output_suffix`, while the implementation plan tells T001/T002 to pass and add `out_suffix: Option<String>`. These plan artifacts disagree on the target API field name, so an implementer could satisfy one artifact while violating the other.

GO-04 is still informal-only, and the same pattern is repeated for IN-06/AC-07, leaving the spec at 26 blue / 3 yellow instead of an all-blue implementation plan. Because these elements are mapped to T007 and kept in the acceptance surface, the plan is still carrying unpromoted design work; either promote the adr-editor rule change to a formal ADR/reference or keep it outside this plan.

The Reassess When item says to decide after D2 implementation whether D1 step 2/4 should be updated to suffix calls, but D1 already uses `--out-suffix d1/d2` and the spec/tasks rely on that as the canonical procedure. This stale future action contradicts the current ADR state and can send implementers looking for a decision that has already been made.

The ADR now makes `--out-suffix d1/d2` part of D1's canonical procedure and explicitly says the depth=1/depth=2 reads are order-free, but the Reassess When entry still says to consider updating D1 step 2/4 to suffix-style calls after D2 is implemented. That is a stale contradiction inside the decision record and leaves readers unsure whether suffix calls are already the accepted procedure or only a future optional update.

D1 still presents the canonical reconnaissance procedure as an ordered list where depth=1 output is read before the depth=2 `type-graph` run, but the immediately following paragraph and the derived spec/plan say the D2 suffix paths make the reads order-free and T005 should remove the old “Read depth=1 BEFORE running depth=2” constraint. This leaves two conflicting prescriptions for the agent pipeline: preserve the ordered read-before-depth2 sequence, or run both suffixed graphs first and read either output later.

D2's rationale still says D1 adopted the sequence `depth=1 -> Read -> depth=2` and that Read timing can cause depth=1 output to be missed, but the same ADR now states that d1/d2 outputs coexist and step 3/5 Read order is free. This also conflicts with T005, which explicitly removes the old read-before-depth2 constraint, leaving contradictory design intent for the agent rewrite.

The canonical reconnaissance order is contradictory. The ADR's D1 numbered procedure says to read the depth=1 output before running the depth=2 graph, but spec/plan require running both graph commands first and then reading both outputs in free order, with T005 explicitly removing the old depth=1-before-depth=2 read constraint. The implementation task can therefore update the agent definition in a way that no longer matches the cited ADR D1. Reconcile the ADR D1 procedure with the spec/plan ordering before using it as the source of truth.

この仕様は Internal pipeline を「8-step」と呼んでいますが、列挙されている要素は 9 個あります (baseline-capture, type-graph depth=1, type-graph depth=2, depth=1 Read, depth=2 Read, catalogue draft, Write, contract-map, type-signals)。同じ不整合が plan.md と impl-plan.json にも繰り返されており、意図したパイプライン形状が曖昧です。

AC-01 stops at `catalogue draft`, but the corresponding scope/task entries say the Internal pipeline rewrite must cover the full 9-step sequence through `Write`, `contract-map`, and `type-signals`. As written, the acceptance gate can pass without verifying the second half of the intended edit.

This ADR still hard-codes `track/items/<id>/...` paths in the decision body (`D1` step 4/5), which conflicts with the track-independent authoring rule for ADRs in this review scope. The decision should refer to the abstract `<layer>-graph-d1/` / `<layer>-graph-d2/` outputs without embedding the track artifact path.

The new reconnaissance step only reads `track/items/<id>/<layer>-graph/index.md` plus linked cluster files, but `sotp track type-graph` can also produce the flat `<layer>-graph.md` artifact when `--cluster-depth 0`. In that mode the agent would skip the actual graph output and make its draft from incomplete reconnaissance; the pipeline needs to branch on cluster depth, as the ADR describes.

`TDDD-Q04` mislabels the example as a struct field / rustdoc field accessor. `StatusOverride::kind` is a normal getter method over a private field, so `--edges fields` would not separate it out and the stated remediation is aimed at the wrong abstraction.

`StatusOverride -->|kind| StatusOverrideKind` is produced by the public accessor method `StatusOverride::kind()` in `libs/domain/src/track.rs`, not by a rustdoc field accessor. As written, this item describes a field/method mismatch that does not exist, so the proposed `--edges fields` split is based on a false premise.

WF-68’s migration plan is internally inconsistent: step (2) makes new ADRs carry a user/review ref, but step (3) blocks red signals without any exemption for the legacy ADRs that step (4) says may be grandfathered. As written, grandfathered ADRs would still fail `verify adr-signals` instead of being allowed through.
